sn1.11:1.1:
1
Thus have I heard. On one occasion the Blessed One was dwelling at Sāvatthī in Jeta's Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika's Park. There the Blessed One addressed the bhikkhus thus: "Bhikkhus!"
sn1.11:1.2: "Venerable sir!" those bhikkhus replied. The Blessed One said this:
sn1.11:1.3:
2
"Once in the past, bhikkhus, a certain devata of the Tāvatiṁsa host was revelling in Nandana Grove, supplied and endowed with the five cords of celestial sensual pleasure, accompanied by a retinue of celestial nymphs. On that occasion he spoke this verse:
sn1.11:1.4:
3
"‘They do not know bliss
Who have not seen Nandana,
The abode of the glorious male devas
Belonging to the host of Thirty.’
sn1.11:1.5:
4
"When this was said, bhikkhus, a certain devata replied to that devata in verse:
